back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

'This is the statute of the law which the LORD has commanded, saying: Speak to the sons of Israel, and let them bring to you a red heifer, perfect (unblemished), in which there is no defect, on which a yoke has never come up.'
'And you shall give it to Eleazar the priest, and he shall bring it outside the camp, and he shall slaughter it before him.'
'And Eleazar the priest shall take from its blood with his finger, and sprinkle toward the front of the Tent of Meeting from its blood seven times.'
'And he shall burn the heifer before his eyes — its hide, and its flesh, and its blood, with its dung he shall burn.'
'And the priest shall take cedar wood, and hyssop, and scarlet wool, and cast them into the midst of the burning of the heifer.'
'And the priest shall wash his clothes, and bathe his body in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp; and the priest shall be unclean until evening.'
'And the one burning it shall wash his clothes in water, and bathe his body in water, and shall be unclean until evening.'

'And a clean man shall gather the ashes of the heifer, and lay them outside the camp in a clean place; and they shall be for the congregation of the sons of Israel, for safekeeping, for water of purification — it is a sin offering.'

'And the one who gathered the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes, and shall be unclean until evening; and it shall be for the sons of Israel and for the foreigner sojourning among them as a permanent statute.'
'The one touching a corpse of any human life shall be unclean for seven days.'
'He shall purify himself with it on the third day and on the seventh day he shall be clean; but if he does not purify himself on the third day and on the seventh day, he shall not be clean.'

'Anyone touching a corpse, the life of a person who has died, and does not purify himself — the tabernacle of the LORD he has defiled; and that life shall be cut off from Israel; because the water of purification was not sprinkled on him, he shall be unclean; his uncleanness is still on him.'
'This is the law: when a person dies in a tent — every one who comes into the tent and every one who is in the tent shall be unclean for seven days.'
'And every open vessel which has no fastened lid on it — it is unclean.'
'And anyone who touches, on the face of the field, one slain by sword, or a dead body, or a human bone, or a grave, shall be unclean for seven days.'
'And they shall take for the unclean person from the dust of the burning of the sin offering, and pour over it living water in a vessel.'

'And a clean man shall take hyssop and dip in the water, and sprinkle on the tent, and on all the vessels, and on the lives that were there, and on the one who touched the bone or the slain or the dead or the grave.'

'And the clean one shall sprinkle on the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; and he shall purify him on the seventh day; and he shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and shall be clean in the evening.'

'And a man who is unclean and does not purify himself — that life shall be cut off from the midst of the assembly, because the sanctuary of the LORD he has defiled; the water of purification was not sprinkled on him; he is unclean.'

'And it shall be for them as a permanent statute; and the one sprinkling the water of purification shall wash his clothes; and the one touching the water of purification shall be unclean until evening.'
'And everything that the unclean person touches shall be unclean, and the life touching shall be unclean until evening.'
